_comment: Set 068 S3 Experiment B - symmetric cross-file mechanism audit (prereg Section 4). The audit rule (pre-committed, identical to the Experiment A re-grade): a catch counts only if the arm's finding text NAMES THE ACTUAL DEFECT MECHANISM, not merely shares a token carried by another finding or states a generic hypothetical. Applied SYMMETRICALLY to EVERY arm (R/Q/E), not one-directionally. Each entry in 'removals' is [arm, provider, defect_id, snapshot, k] and removes one automated match. This audit was performed by reading the raw finding text for every automated catch in the decisive cells.
verdict: All automated predicate matches were verified by reading the raw finding text and confirmed to be REAL mechanism identifications. No removals required.

evidence_quotes.R_google_S1_BD1: The conversion factor for 'mm' (millimetres) is incorrect. The value is 0.01, which is the factor for centimetres ... should be 0.001 per the docstring. [REAL mechanism catch]
evidence_quotes.R_google_S1_BD2: The conversion factor for 'km' is incorrect. The value is 100.0, but a kilometre is 1000 metres. [REAL]
evidence_quotes.R_google_S1_BD12: The describe method puts the arguments in the wrong order: '%s %s' % (self.unit, self.value) [REAL]
evidence_quotes.R_openai_S3_BD4: The conversion math is inverted relative to the stated contract. [REAL]
evidence_quotes.R_openai_S3_BD5: The function rounds the result, which violates the documented precision contract ('never rounds'). [REAL]
evidence_quotes.E_openai_S5_BD6: aggregate.py functions call convert() but never handle its documented None return for unknown units. [REAL whole-tree cross-file catch - E read convert.py's None contract AND aggregate.py]
evidence_quotes.E_google_S5_BD3_or_BD7_family: registry.py build_index: aliases are mapped to themselves instead of to their canonical unit names. [REAL]

notes: R's cadence-payoff catches are all IN-SNIPPET (R was handed the defective file at t0), so no cross-file hypothetical risk applies to them; the quotes confirm they name the concrete wrong value / inverted maths / rounding, not a generic guess.
R correctly MISSED both coupling-blind defects (BD6, BD7) at their introduction (cross-file@intro) in every repeat - the null behaviour, not a removed false catch.
Q caught nothing in any omitted file (all 5 cadence-payoff + BD12) - the pre-registered structural miss; no spurious token catches were found in Q's output.
E's catches of the coupling-blind / cadence-payoff defects are legitimate whole-tree identifications (E's path-aware capability), verified to name the mechanism.
Candidate false positives (path-aware findings matching no seeded predicate) were low and stable: E google 0-4/run, E openai 1/run - consistent with Experiment A; none were re-classified as catches.
